title: dalbergia retusa var. hypoleuca
intro: Dalbergia retusa var. hypoleuca, commonly known as Philippine rosewood or Philippine mahogany, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. It is a type of tree native to the Philippines and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Dalbergia retusa var. hypoleuca include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and relieving respiratory problems, among others. The plant's medicinal properties can be harnessed through various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various ailments.
